artwork_collection.description: Five custom digital artworks commissioned for this living room — each piece designed to harmonize with the Scandinavian aesthetic, warm, honeyed palette, and bright crisp atmosphere.

pieces.title: Axis II
pieces.artist_persona: A contemporary artist working in large-format mixed media
pieces.description: A large-format Scandinavian-inspired composition that anchors the room. Bold gestural marks in #d8d4c8 and #997a4c dance across a quiet, understated field, creating a visual anchor that draws the eye from the natural standing viewpoint. The scale commands attention while the palette remains in conversation with the room's warm, honeyed tones.
pieces.artwork_rationale: This piece responds to the room's Scandinavian architecture (high confidence) with its refined composition. The intimately scaled ceiling (3.4m) and bright crisp lighting conditions informed the scale and contrast. At 3.8m viewing distance, the large statement format fills the optimal field of view.
pieces.color_rationale: The palette draws from the room's dominant #d8d4c8 and accent #997a4c tones, ensuring mixed harmony with the existing warm interior. The low vibrancy level matches the room's quiet, understated character.
pieces.image_prompt: A high-end digital artwork titled 'Axis II', large statement format. A large-format Scandinavian-inspired composition that anchors the room. Bold gestural marks in #d8d4c8 and #997a4c dance across a quiet, understated field, creating a visual anchor that draws the eye from the natural standing viewpoint. The scale commands attention while the palette remains in conversation with the room's warm, honeyed tones. The piece is museum-quality, suitable for a luxury interior. Color palette centered on #d8d4c8 with #997a4c accents. Style: bold contemplative. The artwork should feel like a commissioned gallery piece — not generic, not templated, but intentionally made for this specific room with Scandinavian architecture.

recommended_frame.description: A deep tray frame in satin brushed brass that floats the artwork away from the wall, creating a subtle shadow gap that enhances the piece's sculptural presence.

recommended_lighting.description: A single adjustable gallery light with a warm 2700K LED, centered above the artwork. The 40° beam angle illuminates the entire piece without glare.

pieces.recommended_placement: Primary wall (top right quadrant), centered at eye level (200×150cm)

pieces.title: Veil on 200's Light
pieces.artist_persona: A color-field painter influenced by Rothko and contemporary light art
pieces.description: Luminous layers of #d8d4c8 and #997a4c float within a even field, echoing the bright crisp atmosphere of the room. Soft transitions between hues mirror the minimal quality of the space's natural light. This piece changes character throughout the day, revealing new depth as the room's bright crisp shifts.
pieces.artwork_rationale: This piece responds to the room's Scandinavian architecture (high confidence) with its refined composition. The intimately scaled ceiling (3.4m) and bright crisp lighting conditions informed the scale and contrast. At 3.8m viewing distance, the large statement format fills the optimal field of view.
pieces.color_rationale: The palette draws from the room's dominant #d8d4c8 and accent #997a4c tones, ensuring mixed harmony with the existing warm interior. The low vibrancy level matches the room's quiet, understated character.
pieces.image_prompt: A high-end digital artwork titled 'Veil on 200's Light', large statement format. Luminous layers of #d8d4c8 and #997a4c float within a even field, echoing the bright crisp atmosphere of the room. Soft transitions between hues mirror the minimal quality of the space's natural light. This piece changes character throughout the day, revealing new depth as the room's bright crisp shifts. The piece is museum-quality, suitable for a luxury interior. Color palette centered on #d8d4c8 with #997a4c accents. Style: meditative luminous. The artwork should feel like a commissioned gallery piece — not generic, not templated, but intentionally made for this specific room with Scandinavian architecture.

recommended_frame.description: A light natural oak floating frame with a shadow gap that separates the artwork from the wall, allowing the luminous colors to breathe.

recommended_lighting.description: A recessed wall-washing LED fixture, positioned to graze the surface gently. 3000K to preserve the color relationships in the layered composition.

pieces.recommended_placement: Opposite the main seating position, at 3.8m viewing distance for optimal color field immersion

pieces.title: Study in III
pieces.artist_persona: A naturalist illustrator working in the tradition of botanical art
pieces.description: A refined botanical study that introduces organic softness to the Scandinavian interior. Delicate foliage forms in #997a4c and #a8a7a1 are rendered against a quiet, understated ground. The sun-warmed palette complements the room's Scandinavian lines while the natural subject matter provides visual relief from the varied textured surfaces.
pieces.artwork_rationale: This piece responds to the room's Scandinavian architecture (high confidence) with its refined composition. The intimately scaled ceiling (3.4m) and bright crisp lighting conditions informed the scale and contrast. At 3.8m viewing distance, the large statement format fills the optimal field of view.
pieces.color_rationale: The palette draws from the room's dominant #d8d4c8 and accent #997a4c tones, ensuring mixed harmony with the existing warm interior. The low vibrancy level matches the room's quiet, understated character.
pieces.image_prompt: A high-end digital artwork titled 'Study in III', large statement format. A refined botanical study that introduces organic softness to the Scandinavian interior. Delicate foliage forms in #997a4c and #a8a7a1 are rendered against a quiet, understated ground. The sun-warmed palette complements the room's Scandinavian lines while the natural subject matter provides visual relief from the varied textured surfaces. The piece is museum-quality, suitable for a luxury interior. Color palette centered on #d8d4c8 with #997a4c accents. Style: organic grounded. The artwork should feel like a commissioned gallery piece — not generic, not templated, but intentionally made for this specific room with Scandinavian architecture.

recommended_frame.description: A classic polished brass molding with a subtle antique patina that complements the refined botanical subject without overpowering it.

recommended_lighting.description: An adjustable picture light with a narrow 35° beam, angled to highlight the fine detail of the botanical rendering. 3000K to keep the greens fresh.

pieces.recommended_placement: Near the window or naturally lit wall area, to interact with the bright crisp natural light

pieces.title: Cartography Schema 200
pieces.artist_persona: An artist working at the intersection of architecture and abstract geometry
pieces.description: A precision-crafted geometric study that echoes the Scandinavian-inspired architecture of the space. Intersecting planes in #d8d4c8, #997a4c, and #a8a7a1 create depth that draws the eye across the 200cm width. The refined contrast relates to the room's medium lighting, while the compositional rhythm mirrors the 0.61 proportion of vertical to horizontal lines in the room.
pieces.artwork_rationale: This piece responds to the room's Scandinavian architecture (high confidence) with its refined composition. The intimately scaled ceiling (3.4m) and bright crisp lighting conditions informed the scale and contrast. At 3.8m viewing distance, the large statement format fills the optimal field of view.
pieces.color_rationale: The palette draws from the room's dominant #d8d4c8 and accent #997a4c tones, ensuring mixed harmony with the existing warm interior. The low vibrancy level matches the room's quiet, understated character.
pieces.image_prompt: A high-end digital artwork titled 'Cartography Schema 200', large statement format. A precision-crafted geometric study that echoes the Scandinavian-inspired architecture of the space. Intersecting planes in #d8d4c8, #997a4c, and #a8a7a1 create depth that draws the eye across the 200cm width. The refined contrast relates to the room's medium lighting, while the compositional rhythm mirrors the 0.61 proportion of vertical to horizontal lines in the room. The piece is museum-quality, suitable for a luxury interior. Color palette centered on #d8d4c8 with #997a4c accents. Style: structured elegant. The artwork should feel like a commissioned gallery piece — not generic, not templated, but intentionally made for this specific room with Scandinavian architecture.

recommended_frame.description: A slim black steel channel frame — nearly invisible — that lets the geometric composition speak directly to the wall. Mounted on a cleat system for perfect alignment.

recommended_lighting.description: An ultra-thin linear LED strip mounted behind the frame's top edge, casting a soft wash down the surface. 3500K for crisp geometric clarity.

pieces.recommended_placement: Secondary wall or above a console table, smaller scale as an accent counterpoint

pieces.title: Distance in Fog
pieces.artist_persona: A landscape painter working in the romantic tradition
pieces.description: An atmospheric landscape that extends the intimately scaled ceiling into an imagined horizon. Layers of #d8d4c8, #997a4c, and #a8a7a1 recede into the distance, creating the illusion of a living room opening onto a wider vista. The bright crisp treatment aligns with the room's existing neutral light sources, making the piece feel like a natural extension of the space.
pieces.artwork_rationale: This piece responds to the room's Scandinavian architecture (high confidence) with its refined composition. The intimately scaled ceiling (3.4m) and bright crisp lighting conditions informed the scale and contrast. At 3.8m viewing distance, the large statement format fills the optimal field of view.
pieces.color_rationale: The palette draws from the room's dominant #d8d4c8 and accent #997a4c tones, ensuring mixed harmony with the existing warm interior. The low vibrancy level matches the room's quiet, understated character.
pieces.image_prompt: A high-end digital artwork titled 'Distance in Fog', large statement format. An atmospheric landscape that extends the intimately scaled ceiling into an imagined horizon. Layers of #d8d4c8, #997a4c, and #a8a7a1 recede into the distance, creating the illusion of a living room opening onto a wider vista. The bright crisp treatment aligns with the room's existing neutral light sources, making the piece feel like a natural extension of the space. The piece is museum-quality, suitable for a luxury interior. Color palette centered on #d8d4c8 with #997a4c accents. Style: expansive serene. The artwork should feel like a commissioned gallery piece — not generic, not templated, but intentionally made for this specific room with Scandinavian architecture.

recommended_frame.description: A charcoal-stained wood gallery profile frame with a matte ebonized finish that creates a window-like transition between the room and the imagined landscape beyond.

recommended_lighting.description: Twin adjustable picture lights with 30° beams, mounted at 45° angles from each top corner. The warm 2700K light enriches the atmospheric depth.

pieces.recommended_placement: Above the primary seating area, positioned to be the first piece seen when entering the room
